title: Consuming evict(self, client) prevents post-eviction use of key handles — keep it
description: All key types take self by value in evict(), making it impossible to use a key handle after eviction. The alternative (&mut self) would allow calling evict() then sign(), which the HSM server would reject with a runtime error. By consuming self, the incorrect sequence is a compile-time error. A future contributor might want to add &self evict() for 'ergonomics'; reject because the current design moves the bug from runtime to compile time.
